# Ramplitude

> Unofficial Ruby gem that posts events to Amplitude analytics. Port of the
> official Amplitude Python SDK with a Sink/Uploader split so events can be
> buffered in-memory, in Redis, or anywhere else, then flushed in-process or
> from a background job (Sidekiq, ActiveJob, cron, etc.).

Not affiliated with Amplitude, Inc. "Amplitude" is their trademark.

## When to use this gem

- The host application needs to send analytics events to Amplitude.
- The codebase is Ruby (3.0+).
- For Rails apps that must keep request paths free of HTTP I/O, prefer the
  Redis + ActiveJob recipe below over the default in-process uploader.

## Install

Add to the host application's Gemfile:

```ruby
gem "ramplitude"
gem "redis"            # only if using the Redis sink
gem "connection_pool"  # recommended for the Redis sink in multi-threaded apps
```

Then `bundle install`. The gem has no runtime dependencies beyond stdlib.

## Configuration surface (Ramplitude::Config)

| Option              | Type     | Default                | Purpose |
| ------------------- | -------- | ---------------------- | --- |
| `api_key`           | String   | required               | Amplitude project API key |
| `flush_queue_size`  | Integer  | 200                    | Events per batch |
| `flush_interval_ms` | Integer  | 10_000                 | Consumer wake-up interval (in-process uploader only) |
| `flush_max_retries` | Integer  | 12                     | Per-event retry cap before drop |
| `server_zone`       | Symbol   | `:us`                  | `:us` or `:eu` |
| `use_batch`         | Boolean  | `false`                | Use `/batch` endpoint instead of `/2/httpapi` |
| `server_url`        | String   | derived                | Override endpoint entirely |
| `min_id_length`     | Integer  | nil                    | Override Amplitude's 5-char minimum |
| `on_event`          | Proc     | nil                    | `->(event, code, message)` callback for every result |
| `sink`              | Sink     | `Sinks::InMemory.new`  | Where events sit before upload |
| `uploader`          | Uploader | `Uploaders::Threaded.new` | Who drains the sink |
| `plan`              | Plan     | nil                    | Tracking plan metadata |
| `ingestion_metadata`| object   | nil                    | Ingestion metadata |
| `logger`            | Logger   | stdout                 | Where warnings/errors go |

## Recipe 1 — Default in-process (scripts, workers, small services)

```ruby
require "ramplitude"

amplitude = Ramplitude::Client.new(api_key: ENV.fetch("AMPLITUDE_API_KEY"))

amplitude.track("Page Viewed", user_id: "u-1", event_properties: { path: "/" })
amplitude.identify(user_id: "u-1") { |i| i.set(:plan, "pro").add(:logins, 1) }
amplitude.revenue(user_id: "u-1", price: 9.99, quantity: 1, product_id: "sku-42")
amplitude.set_group("org_id", "15", user_id: "u-1")

amplitude.flush.wait   # optional; at_exit also flushes
amplitude.shutdown
```

Threading note: NO threads spawn until the first `track`/`identify`/etc.
call. After that, 1 consumer + 4 worker threads (configurable via
`Uploaders::Threaded.new(pool_size: N)`) drain the in-memory sink in the
background.

## Recipe 2 — Module-level singleton (one Amplitude project per process)

```ruby
# config/initializers/ramplitude.rb (Rails) or boot script
Ramplitude.configure do |c|
  c.api_key   = ENV.fetch("AMPLITUDE_API_KEY")
  c.server_zone = :eu                # if applicable
  c.use_batch = true                 # better for high-volume
end

# Anywhere in the app:
Ramplitude.track("Checkout Completed", user_id: user.id.to_s,
                 event_properties: { cents: order.total_cents })
```

## Recipe 3 — Production Rails: Redis sink + ActiveJob drainer

Keeps web requests free of any HTTP I/O. Web workers push events to Redis;
a periodic ActiveJob bulk-uploads them.

```ruby
# config/initializers/ramplitude.rb
require "ramplitude"
require "connection_pool"

REDIS_POOL = ConnectionPool.new(size: 10, timeout: 5) do
  Redis.new(url: ENV.fetch("REDIS_URL"))
end

Ramplitude.configure do |c|
  c.api_key   = ENV.fetch("AMPLITUDE_API_KEY")
  c.sink      = Ramplitude::Sinks::Redis.new(redis: REDIS_POOL, key: "ramp:events")
  c.uploader  = Ramplitude::Uploaders::Null.new   # web does not drain
  c.use_batch = true
end
```

```ruby
# app/jobs/ramplitude_flush_job.rb
class RamplitudeFlushJob < ApplicationJob
  queue_as :ramplitude

  def perform(max_batches: 50)
    Ramplitude::BulkFlusher.new(
      api_key: ENV.fetch("AMPLITUDE_API_KEY"),
      sink:    Ramplitude::Sinks::Redis.new(redis: REDIS_POOL, key: "ramp:events"),
      config:  Ramplitude::Config.new(use_batch: true, flush_queue_size: 1000),
    ).drain(max_batches: max_batches)
  end
end
```

Schedule with whatever scheduler the host app already uses (solid_queue
recurring, sidekiq-cron, whenever, etc.) — every 60 seconds is usually fine.

The Redis sink accepts a bare `Redis` client OR a `ConnectionPool` — it
detects via `respond_to?(:with)` and wraps a bare client in a `NullPool` so
every Redis command uniformly checks out a connection. Concurrent
RamplitudeFlushJob runs are safe — `pull` uses a Lua script (`ZRANGEBYSCORE` +
`ZREM`) for atomic batch dequeue.

## Event shapes

`track` accepts three forms:

```ruby
client.track("Button Clicked", user_id: "u-1", event_properties: { color: "red" })
client.track("Button Clicked") { |e| e.user_id = "u-1"; e.platform = "web" }
client.track(Ramplitude::Event.new(event_type: "Click", user_id: "u-1"))
```

`identify` builds user-property operations:

```ruby
client.identify(user_id: "u-1") do |i|
  i.set        :plan, "pro"
  i.set_once   :first_seen_at, Time.now.iso8601
  i.add        :login_count, 1            # numeric only
  i.append     :badges, "early-adopter"
  i.prepend    :recent_pages, "/home"
  i.pre_insert :tags, "vip"
  i.post_insert :tags, "trial"
  i.remove     :badges, "beta"
  i.unset      :deprecated_field
  i.clear_all                              # nuke everything
end
```

`revenue`:

```ruby
client.revenue(user_id: "u-1",
               price: 9.99, quantity: 2, product_id: "sku-42",
               revenue_type: "purchase", currency: "USD")
```

`set_group` / `group_identify`:

```ruby
client.set_group("org_id", "15", user_id: "u-1")
client.group_identify("org_id", "15", user_id: "u-1") { |i| i.set(:locale, "en-us") }
```

## Plugins

Three pipeline stages: BEFORE → ENRICHMENT → DESTINATION. Return `nil` from
an enrichment plugin to drop the event.

Block sugar for simple cases:

```ruby
client.before { |e| e.platform ||= "web"; e }
client.enrich { |e| e.event_properties&.delete(:email); e }
```

Class form for testable / reusable plugins:

```ruby
class StripPII < Ramplitude::Plugin::EventPlugin
  def initialize; super(Ramplitude::PluginType::ENRICHMENT); end
  def execute(event)
    event.event_properties&.delete(:email)
    event
  end
end
client.add(StripPII.new)
```

## Behaviours an agent should NOT change

1. Don't bypass the Sink — `track` is the only entry point that runs plugins.
2. Don't share a single `Redis` client across many threads without a pool.
3. Don't call `flush.wait` from inside a request — it can block on HTTP.
4. Don't disable gzip unless debugging — bodies >= 1 KiB are compressed
   automatically and Amplitude prefers it.

## Common pitfalls

- Events without `user_id` AND without `device_id` are silently dropped.
- `Identify#add` raises `InvalidEventError` if the value isn't `Numeric`.
- The default in-memory sink loses events on process crash. For durability,
  switch to `Sinks::Redis`.
- The Redis sink ZSET key (`amplitude:events` by default) MUST be unique
  per Amplitude project if multiple clients write to the same Redis.

## Resources

- Repository:   https://github.com/julik/ramplitude
- Port plan:    https://github.com/julik/ramplitude/blob/main/llm/plans/port-plan.md
- Reference:    Amplitude HTTP API v2 — https://amplitude.com/docs/apis/analytics/http-v2
- Batch API:    https://amplitude.com/docs/apis/analytics/batch-event-upload
- Upstream SDK: https://github.com/amplitude/Amplitude-Python
